exercises.title: Dual Containment Verifier
exercises.description: Verify the dual containment condition for CSS code construction.
tasks: Implement H₁·H₂ᵀ mod 2 calculation
Test with Hamming code (should be self-orthogonal)
Test with non-compatible code pairs
Visualize which row pairs commute/anticommute
Report whether CSS construction is valid
hints: Result should be all zeros for valid CSS

exercises.title: CSS Parameter Calculator
exercises.description: Calculate quantum code parameters from classical codes.
tasks: Compute k = k₁ + k₂ - n from parity check matrices
Determine number of stabilizer generators
Calculate code rate k/n
Compare with known CSS codes (Steane, etc.)
Generate parameter table for various classical codes
hints: k₁ = n - rank(H₁) for classical code
Verify: n - k = number of stabilizers
Include Hamming, repetition, and Golay codes

exercises.title: CSS Stabilizer Generator
exercises.description: Generate stabilizer operators from parity check matrices.
tasks: Convert H₁ rows to Z-type Pauli strings
Convert H₂ rows to X-type Pauli strings
Build actual stabilizer matrices (2ⁿ × 2ⁿ)
Verify all stabilizers commute pairwise
Check that logical states are +1 eigenstates
hints: Z-string: '1' in row → Z at that position
Use np.kron to build full operators

exercises.title: CSS Code from Scratch
exercises.description: Build a complete CSS code from classical linear codes.
tasks: Construct CSS code from BCH or Reed-Muller codes
Implement encoding circuit (conceptual or simulated)
Implement syndrome measurement
Test error correction on random single errors
Compare performance with Steane code
hints: BCH codes have good distance properties
